meta.note: Add exercises by copying the structure. Upload back when ready.

fields.name: Display name
fields.bodyPart: arms | back | chest | core | full_body | legs | shoulders
fields.equipment: none | bodyweight | barbell | dumbbell | cable | machine | kettlebell | resistance_band | cardio_machine | other

compound.name: Barbell Bench Press
compound.description: The foundational horizontal push. Builds chest thickness and pressing strength.
compound.instructions: Lie flat on a bench with feet on the floor. Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width. Unrack and lower to mid-chest with control. Press back to lockout.

compound.name: Incline Barbell Bench Press
compound.description: Upper chest emphasis. Bench set to 30–45 degrees.
compound.instructions: Set bench to 30–45°. Grip slightly wider than shoulders. Lower the bar to upper chest, press back to lockout. Keep feet flat and back in contact with the pad.

compound.name: Decline Barbell Bench Press
compound.description: Lower chest emphasis. Bench set to -30 to -45 degrees.
compound.instructions: Set bench to  -30° to -45°. Grip slightly wider than shoulders. Lower the bar to lower chest, press back to lockout. Keep feet flat and back in contact with the pad.

compound.name: Dumbbell Bench Press
compound.description: Greater range of motion than barbell. Good for identifying and correcting side imbalances.
compound.instructions: Lie flat, dumbbells at chest level. Press up and in, bringing the bells close at the top. Lower with control to a comfortable stretch.

compound.name: Dips
compound.description: Bodyweight vertical push. Lean forward to emphasise chest, stay upright for triceps. Strap a specialized belt on and hang weights from it to turn this into a freeweight exercise.
compound.instructions: Support yourself on parallel bars. Lower your body by bending at the elbows, leaning slightly forward. Push back up to full arm extension.

compound.name: Barbell Deadlift
compound.description: The king of posterior chain movements. Works everything from traps to hamstrings.
compound.instructions: Bar over mid-foot. Hip-width stance. Hinge at hips to grip bar. Brace core, flatten back. Drive through the floor, extend hips and knees simultaneously. Lock out at the top.

compound.name: Barbell Row
compound.description: Horizontal pull. Builds back thickness and helps counteract pressing movements.
compound.instructions: Hinge to roughly 45°. Pull bar to lower chest / upper abdomen. Lead with the elbows. Lower with control.

compound.name: Dumbbell Row
compound.description: Single-arm horizontal pull. Allows full range of motion and helps fix imbalances.
compound.instructions: Brace one hand and knee on a bench. Pull the dumbbell to your hip, keeping elbow close. Lower with control. Full stretch at the bottom.

compound.name: Pull-Up
compound.description: The definitive vertical pull. Overhand grip emphasises width.
compound.instructions: Hang from a bar with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulders. Pull until chin clears the bar, focusing on driving elbows to hips. Lower with control.

compound.name: Chin-Up
compound.description: Vertical pull with supinated grip. Greater bicep contribution than pull-up.
compound.instructions: Hang with a supinated (underhand) grip, hands shoulder-width. Pull until chin clears the bar. Lower with control.

compound.name: Lat Pulldown
compound.description: Cable pull-up substitute. Good for building the lat strength needed for bodyweight pull-ups.
compound.instructions: Grip wide bar with overhand grip. Sit with thighs under pads. Pull bar to upper chest, leading with elbows. Control the return.

compound.name: Cable Row
compound.description: Seated horizontal pull with constant cable tension. Back thickness builder.
compound.instructions: Sit upright at cable station, slight forward lean. Pull handle to lower abdomen, driving elbows back. Hold briefly, return with control.

compound.name: Overhead Press
compound.description: The foundational overhead push. Builds shoulder strength and stability.
compound.instructions: Bar at front rack position. Grip just outside shoulders. Brace everything. Press overhead to full lockout. Return under control.

compound.name: Dumbbell Shoulder Press
compound.description: Overhead press with dumbbells. Greater range of motion and unilateral balance demand.
compound.instructions: Seated or standing. Press from ear level to full lockout. Avoid excessive arching. Lower with control.

compound.name: Arnold Press
compound.description: Rotating dumbbell press that works all three deltoid heads through a wide arc.
compound.instructions: Start with palms facing you, bells at chin level. Rotate hands outward as you press overhead. Reverse on the way down.

compound.name: Barbell Back Squat
compound.description: The foundational lower body movement. Quad-dominant with significant glute and core demand.
compound.instructions: Bar on upper traps, feet shoulder-width. Brace and squat down until thighs parallel or below. Drive through the floor to stand. Knees track over toes.

compound.name: Barbell Front Squat
compound.description: More upright torso than back squat. Greater quad demand, requires significant wrist and shoulder mobility.
compound.instructions: Bar across front delts, elbows high. Squat upright, keeping elbows pointing forward throughout. Drive through the floor.

compound.name: Hack Squat
compound.description: focusing heavily on quads while limiting lower back strain and eliminating the need for stabilization, making them ideal for hypertrophy and beginners
compound.instructions: Place your back against the pad with shoulders under the pads, feet shoulder-width apart on the platform, and unlock the safety handles. Lower the sled slowly until hips are below knees, keeping your back straight, then drive through your heels to return to the starting position.

compound.name: Romanian Deadlift
compound.description: Hip hinge with straight legs. Primary hamstring and glute developer.
compound.instructions: Hold bar at hip level. Hinge back, pushing hips behind you with a slight knee bend. Lower until you feel a deep hamstring stretch. Drive hips forward to return.

compound.name: Bulgarian Split Squat
compound.description: Unilateral squat with rear foot elevated. High quad and glute demand, good for balance.
compound.instructions: Rear foot elevated on a bench. Lower the back knee toward the floor, keeping front shin vertical. Drive through the front heel to stand.

compound.name: Leg Press
compound.description: Machine squat pattern. Lower back load is reduced compared to barbell squat.
compound.instructions: Feet shoulder-width on platform. Lower sled until knees reach 90°. Press through heels to extend. Do not lock knees at top.

compound.name: Hip Thrust
compound.description: Primary glute builder. Bar positioned across the hips.
compound.instructions: Upper back on a bench, bar across hips. Drive hips to ceiling by squeezing glutes. Hold briefly at top. Lower with control.

compound.name: Lunges
compound.description: Unilateral leg exercise. Works quad, glute, and balance simultaneously.
compound.instructions: Step forward into a split stance. Lower back knee toward the floor. Push off the front foot to return. Alternate legs.

compound.name: Barbell Lunges
compound.description: Unilateral leg exercise. Works quad, glute, and balance simultaneously.
compound.instructions: Place barbell over your shoulders like a back squat. Step forward into a split stance. Lower back knee toward the floor. Push off the front foot to return. Alternate legs.

isolation.name: Dumbbell Fly
isolation.description: Chest stretch and squeeze without tricep involvement.
isolation.instructions: Lie flat, dumbbells above chest with slight elbow bend. Lower in a wide arc until you feel a stretch. Squeeze chest to bring bells back together.

isolation.name: Hip Abductors
isolation.description: a seated isolation exercise designed to strengthen the outer hips and glutes by forcing the legs apart against resistance. It effectively targets the gluteus medius, minimus, and tensor fasciae latae (TFL) to improve pelvic stability, enhance hip strength, and shape the outer thighs.
isolation.instructions: Sit with your back against the pad, feet on the rests, and knees against the pads. Push outward slowly, hold for 1–2 seconds, and return to center without letting the weights touch.

isolation.name: Hip Adductors
isolation.description: a seated, isolation exercise designed to strengthen the inner thigh muscles (adductors) by bringing the legs toward the body's midline against resistance. It targets the adductor magnus, longus, and gracilis, improving hip stability and enhancing lower body strength for compound movements like squats.
isolation.instructions: Sit with your back against the pad, adjust the knee pads to the outside, and place your feet on the rest. Squeeze your thighs together against the pads in a controlled, slow motion until they almost touch, then slowly return to the starting position.

isolation.name: Cable Fly
isolation.description: Cable keeps constant tension through the full arc of motion.
isolation.instructions: Set cables high or low depending on chest portion targeted. Step forward, bring hands together in a hugging motion. Squeeze at the peak. Cross hands to get that last flex.

isolation.name: Pec Deck
isolation.description: Machine fly that removes stabilisation demand. Good for learning the chest contraction.
isolation.instructions: Sit upright, arms on pads at chest height. Bring pads together, squeezing at the peak. Return with control.

isolation.name: Straight Arm Pulldown
isolation.description: Isolates the lats without bicep involvement. Good lat mind-muscle connection builder.
isolation.instructions: Stand at cable station. Arms straight with slight elbow bend. Pull bar down to hips in an arc. Squeeze lats at the bottom.

isolation.name: Face Pull
isolation.description: Rear delt and external rotation. Essential for shoulder health when pressing volume is high.
isolation.instructions: Cable set at head height with rope attachment. Palms facing in on the rope. Pull toward your face, elbows high and wide. Externally rotate at the end of the movement.

isolation.name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise
isolation.description: Lateral deltoid isolation. Builds shoulder width.
isolation.instructions: Stand with dumbbells at sides. Raise arms out to the sides to shoulder height. Lead with elbows, slight forward lean. Lower with control.

isolation.name: Dumbbell Front Raise
isolation.description: Anterior deltoid isolation.
isolation.instructions: Hold dumbbells in front of thighs. Raise one or both arms straight ahead to shoulder height. Lower with control.

isolation.name: Reverse Fly
isolation.description: Rear deltoid isolation. Counterbalances heavy pressing work.
isolation.instructions: Hinge forward at the waist. Raise dumbbells out to the sides, leading with elbows. Squeeze rear delts at the top.

isolation.name: Barbell Curl
isolation.description: Classic bicep builder.
isolation.instructions: Stand with shoulder-width underhand grip. Curl bar to shoulder height, keeping elbows close to sides. Squeeze at top. Lower fully.

isolation.name: Dumbbell Curl
isolation.description: Unilateral bicep curl. Allows supination through the movement.
isolation.instructions: Curl from neutral to supinated as you raise. One arm at a time or alternating. Keep elbows stationary.

isolation.name: Hammer Curl
isolation.description: Neutral grip curl. Works brachialis and brachioradialis in addition to biceps.
isolation.instructions: Keep palms neutral throughout the movement. Curl to shoulder height. Lower fully.

isolation.name: Preacher Curl
isolation.description: Bicep curl with the upper arm anchored on a pad. Removes cheating.
isolation.instructions: Rest upper arm on the preacher pad. Curl through full range. Lower slowly — the eccentric is key here.

isolation.name: Tricep Pushdown
isolation.description: Cable tricep isolation. Good for high rep pump work.
isolation.instructions: Cable set high with bar or rope. Elbows close to sides. Extend arms fully, squeezing triceps. Return only to 90°.

isolation.name: Skull Crusher
isolation.description: Lying tricep extension. Excellent mass builder for the long head.
isolation.instructions: Lie on a bench, bar extended above chest. Lower bar toward the forehead by bending elbows. Extend back to lockout.

isolation.name: Overhead Tricep Extension
isolation.description: Stretches the long head of the tricep under load.
isolation.instructions: Hold one dumbbell with both hands overhead. Lower behind the head by bending elbows. Extend back to lockout.

isolation.name: Leg Extension
isolation.description: Quad isolation. Useful for building VMO strength.
isolation.instructions: Adjust machine so the pad sits above the ankles. Extend both legs to full lockout. Squeeze quads at the top. Lower with control.

isolation.name: Leg Curl
isolation.description: Hamstring isolation. Lying or seated.
isolation.instructions: Position pad behind the ankles. Curl heels toward glutes. Squeeze hamstrings at full contraction. Lower fully.

isolation.name: Calf Raise
isolation.description: Gastrocnemius and soleus isolation.
isolation.instructions: Stand with shoulders under the pads, toes on the edge of the platform. Lower heels as far as comfortable. Rise up fully on toes. Hold briefly at top.

isolation.name: Seated Calf Raise
isolation.description: Seated version isolates the soleus more than the standing variation.
isolation.instructions: Sit with pads on the lower thigh. Rise on toes through full range. Lower heels below the platform level.

isolation.name: Glute Kickback
isolation.description: Cable glute isolation. Targets the gluteus maximus directly.
isolation.instructions: Attach ankle strap to cable set low. Stand facing the machine. Extend one leg back until hip fully extended. Squeeze glute at peak. Return with control.

isolation.name: Cable Crunch
isolation.description: Weighted ab flexion. Allows progressive overload for the core.
isolation.instructions: Kneel below a high cable pulley. Hold rope at head level. Flex spine downward, bringing elbows toward knees. Squeeze abs at the bottom.

isolation.name: Pallof Press
isolation.description: Anti-rotation core exercise. Builds stability rather than flexion strength.
isolation.instructions: Stand side-on to a cable machine. Hold the handle at chest level. Press it straight out in front of you. The challenge is resisting rotation.

cardio.name: Treadmill Run
cardio.description: Controlled running on a treadmill. Speed and incline adjustable.
cardio.instructions: Set speed and incline. Maintain an upright posture. Land mid-foot. Use as steady-state cardio or interval training.

cardio.name: Stationary Bike
cardio.description: Low-impact leg cardio. Good for recovery days and knee rehabilitation.
cardio.instructions: Adjust seat height so the knee has a slight bend at full extension. Maintain an upright or slightly forward posture. Pedal at a consistent cadence.

cardio.name: Cycling
cardio.description: Road or outdoor cycling. Great for endurance base building.
cardio.instructions: Maintain a cadence of 70–100 RPM. Keep a neutral spine. Use gears to manage effort rather than grinding at low cadence.

cardio.name: Rowing Machine
cardio.description: Full body cardio with 60% leg drive and 40% upper body pull. Low impact.
cardio.instructions: Drive sequence: legs → lean back → arms. Return: arms → lean forward → legs. Keep the chain level and the stroke smooth.

cardio.name: Jump Rope
cardio.description: High-impact conditioning. Improves coordination and cardiovascular fitness.
cardio.instructions: Keep elbows close to sides, wrists rotating the rope. Land softly on the balls of your feet. Start with basic two-foot jumps.

cardio.name: Sprint Intervals
cardio.description: Short maximum effort sprints with rest intervals. Excellent for conditioning and metabolism.
cardio.instructions: Sprint at maximum effort for the prescribed distance or time. Walk or jog for the rest interval. Stop when form degrades.

cardio.name: Sled Push
cardio.description: Loaded pushing cardio. Builds explosive leg power and cardiovascular endurance simultaneously.
cardio.instructions: Lean into the sled handles at a 45° angle. Drive through the legs in short powerful strides. Keep hips low.

cardio.name: Sled Pull
cardio.description: Loaded pulling cardio. Excellent for hamstrings and posterior chain.
cardio.instructions: Attach a harness or hold straps. Walk or jog backward, driving through the heels. Keep a slight knee bend throughout.

cardio.name: Battle Ropes
cardio.description: Upper body cardiovascular conditioning. Works shoulders, arms, and core.
cardio.instructions: Hold one end of each rope. Create waves by alternating arm movements. Keep core braced and knees slightly bent.

cardio.name: Ski Erg
cardio.description: Upper body pulling cardio. Mimics the double-pole action of cross-country skiing.
cardio.instructions: Stand facing the machine. Pull both handles down and back simultaneously in a powerful downward swing. Use lat and core strength.

cardio.name: Swimming
cardio.description: Full body, zero-impact cardio. Excellent for recovery and aerobic base.
cardio.instructions: Choose a stroke. Focus on technique over speed. Track laps or time.

cardio.name: Assault Bike
cardio.description: Air resistance bike — the harder you push the more resistance it creates. Full body.
cardio.instructions: Pedal and push/pull the handles simultaneously. Pace yourself — the resistance is infinite. Often used for intervals.

cardio.name: Stair Climber
cardio.description: Simulated stair climbing. Higher glute activation than walking.
cardio.instructions: Set a pace you can maintain for the prescribed duration. Keep upright. Drive through the whole foot.

cardio.name: Elliptical
cardio.description: Low-impact full-body cardio machine. Good for those with knee or hip issues.
cardio.instructions: Use the arm handles to engage upper body. Maintain an upright posture. Adjust resistance and incline to increase intensity.

calisthenics.name: Burpee
calisthenics.description: Full body conditioning movement combining a squat, plank, push-up, and jump.
calisthenics.instructions: Squat down, place hands on floor, jump feet back to plank, push-up, jump feet to hands, jump up with arms overhead.

calisthenics.name: Box Jump
calisthenics.description: Explosive lower body plyometric. Builds power and coordination.
calisthenics.instructions: Dip into a quarter squat, swing arms, explode upward. Land softly with bent knees. Step down — don't jump down.

calisthenics.name: Jump Squat
calisthenics.description: Plyometric squat variation. Builds leg power and cardiovascular fitness.
calisthenics.instructions: Squat to parallel. Explode upward to full extension. Land softly by bending knees. Go straight into the next rep.

calisthenics.name: Mountain Climbers
calisthenics.description: Dynamic plank variation. Core stability with high heart rate demand.
calisthenics.instructions: Start in a high plank. Drive one knee toward your chest, then the other in an alternating running motion. Keep hips level.

calisthenics.name: High Knees
calisthenics.description: Running in place with an exaggerated knee drive. Great warm-up or conditioning.
calisthenics.instructions: Drive alternating knees to hip height. Pump arms in opposition. Stay on the balls of your feet.

calisthenics.name: Jumping Jacks
calisthenics.description: Classic conditioning movement. Good for warm-up and active recovery.
calisthenics.instructions: Start with feet together, arms at sides. Jump feet apart while raising arms overhead. Return to start.

calisthenics.name: Push-Up
calisthenics.description: Foundational pushing movement. Many progressions available.
calisthenics.instructions: High plank with hands slightly wider than shoulders. Lower chest to the floor. Press back up. Keep body in a straight line.

calisthenics.name: Diamond Push-Up
calisthenics.description: Close grip push-up variation. Greater tricep demand than standard push-up.
calisthenics.instructions: Form a diamond shape with hands under your chest. Lower chest toward hands. Press back up. Keep elbows close to sides.

calisthenics.name: Plank
calisthenics.description: Isometric core stability hold. Foundation of anti-extension strength.
calisthenics.instructions: Forearms or hands on the floor. Body in a straight line from head to heels. Brace the core, squeeze glutes. Hold.

calisthenics.name: Side Plank
calisthenics.description: Lateral core stability. Works the obliques and hip abductors.
calisthenics.instructions: On one forearm, feet stacked. Raise hips until body is in a straight line. Hold. Keep hips from sagging.

calisthenics.name: V-Up
calisthenics.description: Full ab contraction — upper and lower simultaneously.
calisthenics.instructions: Lie flat. Simultaneously raise arms and legs, bringing hands to feet at the top. Lower with control.

calisthenics.name: Hollow Body Hold
calisthenics.description: Gymnastics core position. Teaches total body tension and anterior core control.
calisthenics.instructions: Lie on your back. Press lower back into the floor. Raise arms overhead and legs off the floor. Hold while maintaining lower back contact.

calisthenics.name: Bear Crawl
calisthenics.description: Quadrupedal movement pattern. Core stability and coordination.
calisthenics.instructions: On all fours with knees hovering an inch off the floor. Crawl forward, moving opposite hand and foot together. Keep hips low and level.

calisthenics.name: Lateral Bound
calisthenics.description: Single-leg lateral jump. Builds lateral power and coordination.
calisthenics.instructions: Jump off one foot to the side. Land on the opposite foot, absorbing force by bending the knee. Bound back immediately.

calisthenics.name: Tuck Jump
calisthenics.description: Explosive vertical jump with knee tuck at peak height.
calisthenics.instructions: Jump vertically as high as possible. At the peak, tuck both knees to your chest. Extend legs to land. Absorb landing with bent knees.

calisthenics.name: Inchworm
calisthenics.description: Dynamic warm-up movement. Hamstring flexibility and shoulder stability.
calisthenics.instructions: Stand and hinge forward to touch the floor. Walk hands out to a plank. Walk feet toward hands. Stand and repeat.

calisthenics.name: Sit ups
calisthenics.description: Fundamental, equipment-free core strengthening exercise performed by lying on your back, bending your knees, and lifting your torso toward your thighs using abdominal muscles. It primarily targets the rectus abdominis, hip flexors, and lower back, helping to increase core strength, flexibility, and posture.
calisthenics.instructions: lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor or anchored, ensuring your lower back stays supported. Cross your arms over your chest or interlock your fingers behind your head, engage your abs, and exhale as you lift your torso toward your knees, slowly rolling down with control.

calisthenics.name: Crunches
calisthenics.description: Foundational core-strengthening exercise primarily targeting the rectus abdominis by lifting the shoulder blades off the floor while keeping the lower back grounded. Unlike sit-ups, they offer a smaller range of motion, reducing hip flexor involvement and focusing tension on the upper abdominal area.
calisthenics.instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat or off the ground crossed, knees bent at a right angle. Place hands behind your head or crossed over your chest. Exhale, engaging your core to lift your shoulder blades off the floor, keeping your lower back down. Crunch your solar plexus down towards your hips by contracting your abdominal muscles, like an accordion. Inhale as you slowly lower with control, preventing neck strain.

stretching.name: Downward Dog
stretching.description: Yoga pose. Stretches hamstrings, calves, and shoulders simultaneously.
stretching.instructions: Start on all fours. Press through palms, lift hips up and back, forming an inverted V. Heels push toward the floor. Head between arms.

stretching.name: Pigeon Pose
stretching.description: Deep hip opener. Stretches the piriformis and hip flexors.
stretching.instructions: From downward dog, bring one knee to the wrist on the same side. Extend the opposite leg behind. Sink hips toward the floor. Hold and breathe.

stretching.name: Warrior I
stretching.description: Standing yoga pose. Hip flexor and thoracic stretch.
stretching.instructions: Lunge forward with the front knee over the ankle. Back foot at 45°. Raise arms overhead. Square hips forward. Hold.

stretching.name: Warrior II
stretching.description: Open stance yoga pose. Hip and inner thigh stretch.
stretching.instructions: Wide stance. Front knee over front ankle, back leg straight. Arms out to the side parallel to the floor. Gaze over the front hand.

stretching.name: Child's Pose
stretching.description: Restorative yoga pose. Gentle lower back and lat stretch.
stretching.instructions: Kneel and sit back on your heels. Extend arms forward on the floor. Rest forehead on the floor. Breathe into your back.

stretching.name: Cat-Cow
stretching.description: Spinal mobility sequence. Flexion and extension of the thoracic and lumbar spine.
stretching.instructions: On all fours. Inhale: drop belly, lift head and tailbone (cow). Exhale: round spine toward ceiling, tuck chin and pelvis (cat).

stretching.name: Seated Forward Fold
stretching.description: Hamstring and lower back stretch.
stretching.instructions: Sit with legs extended. Hinge forward from the hips, reaching for toes. Keep spine long. Hold and breathe.

stretching.name: Supine Twist
stretching.description: Lying spinal rotation. Releases the thoracic spine and piriformis.
stretching.instructions: Lie on your back. Bring one knee across the body to the floor. Extend that arm out to the side. Look in the opposite direction.

stretching.name: Bridge Pose
stretching.description: Hip flexor stretch combined with light glute activation.
stretching.instructions: Lie on your back, knees bent. Press through the feet to lift hips. Clasp hands under your back. Hold and breathe into the chest.

stretching.name: Lizard Pose
stretching.description: Deep hip flexor and groin stretch. Good for athletic populations.
stretching.instructions: From a lunge, bring front foot outside the hand. Lower the back knee to the floor. Sink hips forward and down. Optional: drop to forearms.

stretching.name: Standing Quad Stretch
stretching.description: Classic quad stretch. Essential post-run and post-leg day.
stretching.instructions: Stand on one leg. Pull the other foot to your glute, keeping knees together. Hold onto a wall for balance if needed.

stretching.name: Standing Hamstring Stretch
stretching.description: Hip hinge-based hamstring stretch.
stretching.instructions: Stand with feet together. Hinge forward at the hips, reaching for the floor. Keep a micro-bend in the knees. Hold at the point of tension.

stretching.name: Hip Flexor Stretch
stretching.description: Kneeling lunge stretch targeting the iliopsoas.
stretching.instructions: Kneel with one knee on the floor. Shift hips forward until a stretch is felt in the front of the hip. Keep torso upright. Hold.

stretching.name: Chest Opener
stretching.description: Counteracts the forward rounding from pressing and desk work.
stretching.instructions: Clasp hands behind your back. Squeeze shoulder blades together and lift arms slightly. Open chest toward the ceiling. Hold.

stretching.name: Cross Body Shoulder Stretch
stretching.description: Posterior shoulder and upper back stretch.
stretching.instructions: Bring one arm across your body at shoulder height. Use the other arm to pull it closer to your chest. Hold.

stretching.name: Doorway Chest Stretch
stretching.description: Passive pec and anterior shoulder stretch using a doorframe.
stretching.instructions: Place forearm against a doorframe at 90°. Step forward until a stretch is felt across the chest. Hold. Adjust arm height to target different portions.

stretching.name: Calf Stretch
stretching.description: Gastrocnemius and soleus stretch. Essential for runners.
stretching.instructions: Place hands against a wall. Step one foot back. For gastroc: keep back leg straight. For soleus: bend the back knee. Lean forward.

stretching.name: Seated Piriformis Stretch
stretching.description: Targets the piriformis and external hip rotators.
stretching.instructions: Sit in a chair. Cross one ankle over the opposite knee. Gently press the crossed knee downward. Lean forward for a deeper stretch.

stretching.name: Thoracic Extension on Foam Roller
stretching.description: Mobilises the thoracic spine into extension. Counters desk posture.
stretching.instructions: Place foam roller perpendicular to the spine at mid-back. Support your head. Extend over the roller. Move roller up and down the mid-back.

stretching.name: Neck Side Stretch
stretching.description: Stretches the upper trapezius and scalenes.
stretching.instructions: Sit or stand. Tilt your ear toward your shoulder. Hold. For a deeper stretch, gently place one hand on the side of the head and apply light pressure.

stretching.name: Figure Four Stretch
stretching.description: Supine glute and piriformis stretch.
stretching.instructions: Lie on your back, knees bent. Cross one ankle over the opposite knee. Pull both legs toward your chest. Hold.

stretching.name: Lat Stretch
stretching.description: Overhead lat and shoulder stretch.
stretching.instructions: Reach one arm overhead and hold onto something fixed. Lean away. Alternatively, hang from a bar and shift your weight to one side.

cooldown.name: Diaphragmatic Breathing
cooldown.description: Activates the parasympathetic nervous system. Accelerates recovery.
cooldown.instructions: Lie on your back. One hand on chest, one on belly. Inhale for 4 into the belly. Hold for 4. Exhale for 6. Repeat.

cooldown.name: Box Breathing
cooldown.description: 4-4-4-4 breathing pattern for nervous system regulation.
cooldown.instructions: Inhale for 4 counts. Hold for 4. Exhale for 4. Hold for 4. Repeat for 4–8 cycles.

cooldown.name: Supine Savasana
cooldown.description: Total relaxation. Final resting pose from yoga. Allows the nervous system to downregulate.
cooldown.instructions: Lie flat on your back, arms at sides, palms up. Close your eyes. Relax every muscle. Focus on breathing. Hold for 2–5 minutes.

cooldown.name: Walking
cooldown.description: Light walking to gradually lower heart rate after intense effort.
cooldown.instructions: Walk at a comfortable pace. Allow breathing to normalise. Continue until heart rate approaches resting.

cooldown.name: Foam Rolling — Quads
cooldown.instructions: Lie face down with roller under the quads. Slowly roll from hip to knee. Pause on tender spots for 20–30 seconds.

cooldown.name: Foam Rolling — IT Band
cooldown.description: Self-myofascial release for the iliotibial band.
cooldown.instructions: Lie on one side with roller under the outer thigh. Roll from hip to knee. Adjust body angle to find tender spots.

cooldown.name: Foam Rolling — Upper Back
cooldown.description: Self-myofascial release for the thoracic spine and upper back.
cooldown.instructions: Place roller perpendicular to the spine at mid-back. Support your head. Slowly roll up and down the upper back. Pause on tight areas.
